title: Shell Signs Long-Term Agreement for Sustainable Aviation Fuel with Green Sky Capital
content:
Shell has signed a long-term agreement with Green Sky Capital to purchase 100% of the output from Egyptâ€™s first commercial-scale Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) plant. This offtake agreement provides the necessary commercial certainty for investors to proceed with the project, which is expected to commence operations by the end of 2027.

Once built, the facility aims to produce up to 145,000 tonnes of SAF annually, alongside bionaphtha and biopropane, resulting in a reduction of up to 500,000 tons of CO2 equivalent emissions per year. Geoff Mansfield, Shellâ€™s VP of Low Carbon Fuels, stated that securing the plant's full output strengthens Shellâ€™s global supply network. This move supports Shell's strategy to scale SAF availability, building on its position as a leading global trader with nearly 20% of total SAF sales in Europe and North America in 2024.
author: Analysts Account
